meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve no marketing skills (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no marketing skills. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup for Freelancers: Overcome No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Stop guessing your client's growth. Learn how to implement professional analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to solve your lack of marketing expertise.
content.answer_block: You don't need a marketing degree to prove your app's value. By automating event tracking with Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform raw data into actionable insights. This setup bridges the 'marketing gap' by giving you clear, data-backed performance metrics to show clients, ensuring your work directly drives their revenue.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your project deadline, and your client just asked the one question that haunts every freelancer: 'How do we know if this is working?' You’re a builder, not a growth hacker, and that looming gap in your marketing knowledge feels like a liability. You’ve built the engine, but you don’t know how to track the mileage.

It’s time to stop treating analytics as an afterthought. By integrating automated tracking into your development workflow, you turn data collection into a standard feature. We’re going to use Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent implementation, ensuring you deliver professional-grade analytics without ever needing to touch a complex marketing dashboard.

steps.title: Define Your Conversion Events
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three key user actions that signify value. This prevents 'data bloat' and keeps your analytics focused.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project schema and generate a JSON tracking map for 3 core conversion events: User Signup, Premium Upgrade, and Feature Completion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a tracking map based on my project schema.json'

steps.title: Implement the Tracking Wrapper
steps.description: Create a centralized event-tracking service to avoid hardcoding analytics calls throughout your UI, making maintenance trivial.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a robust, decoupled AnalyticsService class that handles event queuing and failsafe logging for the defined conversion events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a singleton AnalyticsService in src/services'

steps.title: Automate Event Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to inject tracking hooks into your existing components based on the mapping generated in Step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan the component directory and identify UI triggers for the events defined in the tracking map. Prepare a script to inject the AnalyticsService calls.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply tracking hooks to all identified components'

steps.title: Configure Data Visualization Middleware
steps.description: Set up a lightweight middleware that captures metadata (IP, user agent) to provide context for every event tracked.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that intercepts API requests and attaches session context to every analytics event sent to the backend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create middleware for event metadata attachment'

steps.title: Validation and Reporting Loop
steps.description: Automate a testing suite that verifies event firing every time a deployment occurs, ensuring your data pipeline never breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite that mocks event firing and validates that the payload structure matches our tracking map schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate test suite for analytics verification'

pro_tips: Always use a 'no-op' analytics mode in local development to avoid polluting your production data with test clicks.
Tag your events with the specific version of your app to track how features perform across different deployment cycles.
Never send PII (Personally Identifiable Information) to your analytics provider; hash user IDs before transmission.
Implement a 'heartbeat' event that fires every 60 seconds to measure actual session duration, not just page views.
Use a custom 'error' event to capture frontend exceptions, giving you a proactive way to troubleshoot client issues.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics pipeline is live, set up a simple dashboard (like Metabase or Looker) to visualize the data. Your next task is to schedule a 'Data Review' meeting with your client to present these insights as part of your value-add service.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start delivering measurable results that keep clients coming back for more. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
